title: elymus arenarius subsp. sabulosus
intro: Elymus arenarius subsp. sabulosus, also known as beach wildrye, is a subspecies of grass native to coastal areas in the Northern Hemisphere. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Elymus arenarius subsp. sabulosus include its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer, as well as its ability to improve digestion and boost the immune system. Elymus arenarius subsp. sabulosus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
